[Test Improver] test: replace gc.collect+sleep teardown with shutil.rmtree(ignore_errors=True)

Closes #185

Replace fragile tearDown patterns (gc.collect + time.sleep) used to work
around Windows file-lock timing issues with TemporaryDirectory. Switch to
tempfile.mkdtemp() + shutil.rmtree(ignore_errors=True) which is cleaner,
faster, and avoids unnecessary test latency.

Affected files:
- tests/unit/test_deps.py
- tests/unit/test_env_variables.py
- tests/unit/test_helpers.py (removed empty tearDown)
- tests/unit/test_vscode_adapter.py (4 test classes)
- tests/unit/workflow/test_workflow.py (removed safe_rmdir helper)

All 2261 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Test Improver <test-improver@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Create CI/CD pipeline: test + infrastructure + 8 Lambda service deployment workflows (#123)

* Initial plan

* Create CI/CD pipeline workflows for GitHub Actions

- Add test.yml: CI workflow running on every PR with lint, typecheck, unit tests (80% coverage threshold), integration tests, and Codecov upload
- Add deploy-infrastructure.yml: manual Terraform plan/apply with environment approval
- Add deploy-{product,order,cart,content,discount,notification,audit,integration}-service.yml: Lambda deployment workflows triggered on push to main or manual dispatch, with build/test/package/S3-upload/Lambda-update/smoke-test/rollback/Slack-notify steps
- Add backend/services/order-service/tsconfig.json (was missing, all other services had one)
- All workflows use permissions: contents: read for minimal GITHUB_TOKEN scope

Co-authored-by: NAIM9609 <63161846+NAIM9609@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review comments on CI/CD workflows

- Fix environment naming: development/production → dev/prod to match Terraform convention
- Remove LocalStack from integration tests (tests use aws-sdk-client-mock, no real AWS needed)
- Remove duplicate --coverageThreshold CLI flag; raise branches coverage to 80% in jest.config.json
- Make Lambda update loops resilient: use set+e, track ANY_UPDATED output, exit with failure count
- Change rollback condition to trigger on any_updated==true (not just when all updates succeeded)
- Fix smoke tests to fail on non-2xx/3xx responses (not just 5xx)
- Fix order-service smoke test to use /api/admin/orders?page=1 (admin endpoint, no ?email= required)

Co-authored-by: NAIM9609 <63161846+NAIM9609@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Enhance CI/CD workflows with coverage reporting and improve Etsy integration error handling

* fix: Update Codecov action configuration to include token and remove continue-on-error

G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query (#212)

* G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query

TryGetOutwardNormal was constructing BRepAdaptor_Surface(face) on every
call from DistanceToOut(p,v,...).  In the B4c profile this accumulates to
1,682M instructions (10.1% of total) across 246k calls.

FaceBounds already stores a BRepAdaptor_Surface adaptor per face, built
once in ComputeBounds().  Add a primary TryGetOutwardNormal overload that
takes a const BRepAdaptor_Surface& directly (moving the existing body
there, minus the adaptor construction).  Keep the original TopoDS_Face
overload as a one-line shim for the SurfaceNormal path (~84 calls).

In DistanceToOut(p,v,...) look up intersector.Face(minIndex) in
fFaceBoundsCache via TopoDS_Shape::IsSame() (O(1) TShape pointer
compare) and pass the cached adaptor to the new overload.  A fallback
to on-the-fly construction is retained for any face not found in the
cache (should not occur in practice).

Expected impact: ~10% reduction in instruction count for workloads
dominated by DistanceToOut(p,v) normal queries (B4c calorimeter example).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* Initial plan

* G4OCCTSolid: replace O(N) face lookup with O(1) TShape-pointer index

Migrate model trace sweep framework to ttnn_ops_v5 schema with trace_run provenance and sweep manifest (#40466)

## Summary

- **New DB schema `ttnn_ops_v5`** (from `ttnn_ops_v2_5`):
- **Dropped** `ttnn_argument` table entirely — `full_config_json` on
`ttnn_configuration` is now the sole source of truth for arguments
(structured per-argument columns were never queried)
- **Dropped** `primary_*` projection columns (`primary_dtype`,
`primary_storage_type`, `primary_layout`, `primary_memory_layout`,
`primary_buffer_type`, `primary_shape`) from `ttnn_configuration` —
redundant with `full_config_json`
- **Dropped** `operation_type`, `tensor_input_count` from
`ttnn_operation`; `task_type`, `is_lead_model` from `ttnn_model` —
unused columns
- **Added** `model_name` (UNIQUE) on `ttnn_model` — short lowercase
identifier derived at load time (e.g. `deepseek_v3`, `whisper`), used
for manifest target resolution
- **Added** `trace_run` table — one row per load event, links to
`ttnn_hardware` by FK (not inline columns). No `model_id` — which models
are in a trace is derived, not declared.
- **Added** `trace_run_config` — snapshot of which configurations belong
to a trace (with execution_count aggregation)
- **Added** `trace_run_model` — derived/materialized junction table
populated after load, answering "which models are in trace N?" without a
multi-join
- **Added** auto-disambiguation for `model_name` collisions — appends
`_2`, `_3`, etc. instead of failing the load
- **Sweep manifest** (`model_tracer/sweep_manifest.yaml`): Declarative
YAML controlling which traces and models are reconstructed for CI. Two
independent scope groups (`lead_models` / `model_traced`) prevent config
bleed between CI runs.
- **Loader rewrite** (`load_ttnn_ops_data_v2.py`): Targets
`ttnn_ops_v5`, auto-derives lowercase `model_name` per model, creates
`trace_run` + `trace_run_config` + `trace_run_model` on load,
auto-appends draft registry entries to the manifest. No longer writes to
`ttnn_argument`.
- **Manifest-driven reconstruction**: `reconstruct-manifest` resolves
targets to `{trace_id → model_names}` pairs, reconstructs each trace
filtered to the specified models, and merges with config_hash dedup.
- **CLI additions**: `delete-trace` (FK-safe with exclusive-config
detection), `set-model-name`, `--dry-run` on load, schema validation
against SQL injection.
- **Removed legacy code**: V1 schema branches, `ttnn_argument`
insertion, `primary_*` columns, `format_mesh_placement`,
`extract_tensor_info`, `reconstruct-lead` command,
`load_ttnn_ops_data.py` (v1 loader), `master_config_loader.py` (v1
config loader).
- **CI workflow**: `generate-master-json-from-db` job reconstructs
scoped JSON on ubuntu-latest with DB access; hardware runner consumes
the artifact without DB dependency.

## Key design decisions

- `config_hash` (SHA-256 of op + args + hardware + mesh) is the
deduplication key — same config across traces is linked, not duplicated.
The hash is **computed at trace time by `generic_ops_tracer.py`** and
preserved verbatim through load and reconstruction, so its semantics are
pinned to the tracer's `_normalize_for_hash` logic at the git SHA that
produced the trace.
- `model_name` is derived automatically (HF last segment or first
meaningful path segment) but preserved once set via `set-model-name`
(COALESCE semantics). On collision, auto-disambiguates with `_2`, `_3`,
etc.
- Manifest uses **exact** `model_name` matching (not substring) for
target resolution
- `trace_run` has no `model_id` — which models are in a trace is always
derived from `trace_run_model`

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `tests/sweep_framework/load_ttnn_ops_data_v2.py` | Major rewrite: v5
schema, manifest support, trace_run lifecycle |
| `model_tracer/sweep_manifest.yaml` | New: targets + registry YAML |
| `model_tracer/GUIDE.md` | Rewritten for v5 schema and manifest
workflow |
| `model_tracer/destructively_create_ttnn_ops_schema_v5.sql` | New: v5
DDL |
| `.github/workflows/ttnn-run-sweeps.yaml` | Updated: manifest-driven
reconstruction job |
| `tests/sweep_framework/framework/constants.py` | `LEAD_MODELS` derived
from manifest |
| `tests/sweep_framework/master_config_loader_v2.py` | Minor: updated
imports |
| `tests/sweep_framework/load_ttnn_ops_data.py` | Deleted (v1 loader) |
| `tests/sweep_framework/master_config_loader.py` | Deleted (v1 config
loader) |

## Test plan

- [x] `--dry-run` load against live DB shows correct pre-rollback counts
- [x] `reconstruct-manifest model_traced` produces correct config count
matching main CI
- [x] `reconstruct-manifest lead_models` produces only deepseek_v3
configs (no bleed)
- [x] `resolve-manifest` dry-run shows expected trace IDs per scope
- [ ] CI run on this branch matches main branch test case count (parity
check)

---------

perf: eliminate O(log T + k) sqrt calls per BVH safety query (#209)

* Initial plan

* Eliminate per-node/per-triangle sqrt calls in BVH traversal

Work in squared-distance space throughout PointToMeshDistance traversal
and take a single std::sqrt() in BVHLowerBoundDistance() after traversal
completes. This avoids O(log T + k) sqrt calls per safety query."

perf: add vim.loader bytecode cache and mini.icons

vim.loader.enable() in init.lua:
- Compiles all Lua modules to bytecode on first load
- Cached under ~/.cache/nvim/ and reused on subsequent startups
- Saves ~20-40ms startup time with no behavior change

mini.icons (replaces nvim-web-devicons):
- Pure Lua table lookup vs VimL pattern matching
- 2-5x faster icon resolution (called constantly by neo-tree, lualine,
  tabby, trouble, lspsaga on every render)
- package.preload shim transparently redirects require('nvim-web-devicons')
  so all plugins continue working with zero config changes
- Replace nvim-web-devicons dependency in snacks, trouble, tabby

commit.message:
Make AABB availability a class invariant of G4OCCTSolid (#210)

* Initial plan

* refactor: make AABB availability a class invariant of G4OCCTSolid

- Change fCachedBounds from std::optional<AxisAlignedBounds> to plain
  AxisAlignedBounds (value-initialized)
- Throw std::invalid_argument in ComputeBounds() when the bounding box
  is void instead of setting fCachedBounds = std::nullopt
- Remove kFallbackExtentMin / kFallbackExtentMax fallback constants
- Simplify all consumers of fCachedBounds:
  - AABBLowerBound(): drop !has_value() guard, access fields directly
  - DistanceToIn(p): drop std::isfinite() guard
  - Inside(): drop has_value() conditional
  - GetExtent(), BoundingLimits(), CalculateExtent(): drop fallback branches
- Update doc comments in header for fCachedBounds, AABBLowerBound(),
  ComputeBounds(), and SetOCCTShape()
- Convert FallbackWhenAABBUnavailable test to RejectsShapeWithNoGeometry
  using EXPECT_THROW to verify construction-time rejection

feat(blink): enable auto_insert for completion selection

Tab through items now pre-writes the selected text in the buffer.
Press Enter to confirm, Escape to undo and close the menu.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(blink): suppress completions in comments via sources.default instead of enabled()

Using enabled()=false to block comments also disables ALL blink keymaps,
causing Tab to fall through to consume_fallback_keys which inserts a
whitespace character.

Fix: move the comment detection into sources.default (a function called
per completion request). This keeps keymaps fully active while returning
an empty source list when cursor is inside a treesitter comment capture.

Also fold per_filetype (lua/css/scss/gitcommit) into the same function
so lazydev and sass-variables are also suppressed in comments — they
would otherwise bypass the check via inherit_defaults.

chore(agent): target hr briefing release iteration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(blink): remove comment detection from enabled()

The comment detection was never part of the original config - it was
introduced during this session. It caused enabled() to return false
whenever '--' appeared before the cursor, which blocked blink's Tab
keymap (consume_fallback_keys ran instead), inserting whitespace.

Root cause: the check fired correctly for all states where '--' was
present before the cursor, including the auto-continued comment line
after pressing 'o', even if the user had only partially deleted '--'
when pressing Tab.

The fix is simply to remove the comment detection. Blink completions
in comments are arguably useful anyway.

fix(blink): rewrite Tab chain as explicit single function

The multi-command array chain (select_next -> snippet_forward -> function)
was unreliable because each step returns false independently, making it
hard to reason about when neotab runs. Rewrote as a single function with
explicit priority:

  1. Menu visible  -> select_next() — if it returns false (no items),
     the function also returns false; Tab is still consumed by the keymap
     callback (non-expr), so no space is inserted
  2. Snippet active -> snippet_forward()
  3. Otherwise     -> neotab tabout (bracket escape or indent via act_as_tab)

This guarantees neotab never runs while the menu is visible, regardless
of whether select_next succeeds or fails.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add AbortController cleanup and fix WS error on reconnect

JoinGameContent useEffect: AbortController guards all setState calls
after unmount — previously the async validatePlayerGameState chain could
resolve after the component had navigated away, causing a no-op warning
and potential stale-state bugs.

create-game useEffect: same pattern; fetch itself is passed the signal
so the network request is also cancelled, not just the state updates.

useGameWebSocket: clear error state in onclose so a transient error
doesn't stay visible after the reconnect succeeds. Also inline the
reconnect timeout call for clarity.
